
For colliders, I just made a small low poly version on blender and gave it a mesh render. Just import your plane then make an extremely basic shape to fit the entire thing. Once I finished, I just exported it to unity, attached it to the model of the plane I have, then set the layer to walkthrough. This step is important! Without it, your missile/pipper lockons will not work. The plane also has a funny chance to send you flying if you stand on it (Refer to the Dogfight central April fools [video](https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1193332088540237845/1488946446765133945/1775046467783056.mov?ex=6a19c670&is=6a1874f0&hm=713c63bbae3037343d99f88cae68e51330e2a257511853d0482f9c2d97d7a6df&))  
  
REMEMBER: In the wise words of Reaper, “it's all smoke and mirrors!” when making colliders, do not feel they need to be 1:1 with the model, sometimes it’s alright to have it more inside the model. More people will have fun with your plane if they’re able to fly without noticeable collision issues, especially for the Formation flying fans. Giving that slight wiggle room is better than having it over stretch and causing crashes when people are dangerously merging or flying.

![](data:image/png;base64,)
